raw_text:
Attorney Jones: Sarah, I need you to handle a few things on the Johnson v. Smith case. First, we need to file the motion to compel by March 15th — the discovery deadline is coming up fast and opposing counsel has been stonewalling on the document production requests. Second, we need to schedule Dr. Williams' deposition before the end of March. His expert report is critical for our damages argument. Third, make sure the discovery responses go out by March 22nd. The supplemental interrogatory answers need to include the updated financials the client provided last week.

Sarah Chen: Got it. I'll draft the motion to compel this week and have it on your desk for review by the 12th. I'll reach out to Dr. Williams' office today to coordinate deposition scheduling. And I'll pull together the discovery responses — I already have most of the documents organized.

Attorney Jones: Perfect. Let me know if opposing counsel pushes back on the deposition dates.

From: Attorney Jones <jones@lawfirm.com>
To: Sarah Chen <schen@lawfirm.com>
Cc: David Park <dpark@lawfirm.com>
Subject: Johnson v. Smith — Upcoming Deadlines
Date: Wed, 05 Mar 2026 16:45:00 -0500

Sarah,

Following up on our call earlier. Just want to make sure we're aligned on the Johnson deadlines:

1. Motion to Compel — Must be filed by March 15. I reviewed the draft order and it looks solid. Please finalize the supporting declaration and exhibits.

2. Discovery Responses — Supplemental responses due March 22. Make sure we include the bank statements and revised damage calculations from the client.

Please confirm you have everything you need. Let me know if there are any issues with the document production log.

Thanks,
Jones

Johnson v. Smith — Weekly Case Strategy Meeting
Date: March 5, 2026, 3:00 PM
Attendees: Attorney Jones, Sarah Chen, David Park

Agenda & Discussion:

1. Motion to Compel Status
   - Jones: Draft is in good shape. Sarah will finalize supporting declaration and exhibits.
   - Target filing date: March 15 (court deadline).
   - Jones to review final version by March 13.

2. Dr. Williams Deposition
   - Sarah reached out to Dr. Williams' office. Tentatively scheduled for March 28.
   - Need to prepare deposition outline and document binder by March 26.
   - Jones will handle direct examination.

3. Discovery Responses
   - Supplemental interrogatory answers due March 22.
   - Client provided updated bank records — Sarah incorporating into responses.

4. New Action Item — Spoliation Research
   - Jones: Opposing counsel may have destroyed relevant emails. David, please research case law on spoliation sanctions in this jurisdiction. I need a memo by March 25.
   - David Park: Will do. I'll start with the state rules and recent appellate decisions.

Action Items:
- Sarah Chen: File motion to compel by 3/15
- Sarah Chen: Prepare deposition materials for Dr. Williams by 3/26
- Sarah Chen: Finalize discovery responses by 3/22
- David Park: Research spoliation sanctions, memo due 3/25

From: John Rivera <jrivera@lawfirm.com>
To: Michael Torres <mtorres@lawfirm.com>
Subject: Martinez v. ABC Corp — Summary Judgment Prep
Date: Wed, 05 Mar 2026 09:30:00 -0500

Hi Michael,

Just a reminder that the brief in support of our summary judgment motion in Martinez v. ABC Corp is due on March 20th. Can you draft the statement of facts section? I'll handle the legal argument.

Also, we need to respond to the interrogatories by March 10th. Please review the client's answers and flag any issues before I finalize.

Thanks,
John

Voicemail from John Rivera to Michael Torres, March 5, 2026, 2:15 PM:

Hey Michael, it's John. Quick update on Martinez v. ABC Corp. In addition to the summary judgment brief, I need you to start putting together the witness list. We need to identify all fact witnesses and get their contact info confirmed by March 18th. Also, I spoke with Dr. Patel — our economic damages expert — and we need to coordinate with her on the report timeline. Can you reach out to her office and set up a call for next week? Her assistant's name is Linda. Thanks.

Martinez v. ABC Corp — Client Meeting Notes
Date: March 5, 2026, 4:00 PM
Attendees: John Rivera (Attorney), Michael Torres (Associate), Maria Martinez (Client)

Privileged & Confidential — Attorney-Client Communication

1. Settlement Discussions
   - Client reviewed ABC Corp's settlement offer of $150,000. Client rejects — wants to proceed to trial.
   - Rivera: We'll send a formal rejection letter to opposing counsel by March 12.

2. Sanctions Motion
   - ABC Corp's counsel failed to produce the internal safety audit reports despite two court orders.
   - Rivera: Michael, draft a sanctions motion. Filing deadline is March 18.
   - Torres: I'll model it on the sanctions motion we filed in the Henderson case.

3. Trial Preparation
   - Client available for witness prep sessions starting March 23.
   - Discussed key testimony points (privileged).

Action Items:
- John Rivera: Send settlement rejection letter by 3/12
- Michael Torres: Draft sanctions motion by 3/18

From: Office Manager <admin@lawfirm.com>
To: All Attorneys <attorneys@lawfirm.com>
Subject: Weekly Deadline Reminder — Week of March 9
Date: Thu, 06 Mar 2026 08:00:00 -0500

Good morning,

Here is this week's deadline summary across active matters:

Johnson v. Smith:
- Motion to compel filing deadline: March 15 (Sarah Chen handling)
- Supplemental discovery responses due: March 22

Martinez v. ABC Corp:
- Interrogatory responses due: March 10 (Michael Torres reviewing)
- Summary judgment brief due: March 20

Please make sure all deadlines are tracked in the case management system.

Best,
Karen Liu
Office Manager

Attorney Jones: Hey David, how was your weekend?

David Park: Pretty good, thanks. Took the kids to the zoo. The weather was great for once.

Attorney Jones: Nice. We should plan a firm outing sometime this spring. Maybe bowling or something.

David Park: Yeah, that'd be fun. Last year's holiday party was a good time.

Attorney Jones: Agreed. Alright, I'll let you get back to it. Talk later.

David Park: Sounds good. See you at lunch.
